steps.title: Eliminate by direction
steps.content: The graph falls from left to right, so the slope is negative. Choices A and B have positive slopes, so eliminate them. Now only C and D are possible.

steps.title: Use the marked points
steps.content: The graph passes through $(0,-5)$ and $(7,-8)$. Put those points into a Desmos table and run $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=-\frac{3}{7}$ and $b=-5$, so the equation is $f(x)=-\frac{3}{7}x-5$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: If using Desmos, graph the four choices and compare them to the image; only choice C overlaps the graphed line. Algebraically, choice D is too steep because its slope is $-\frac{7}{3}$ instead of $-\frac{3}{7}$. Therefore choice C, $f(x)=-\frac{3}{7}x-5$, is correct.
